 {"id":"bd-pqg","title":"Create Console User Guide documentation","description":"## Purpose
Write a comprehensive user guide for the console feature, covering setup, configuration, and common usage patterns.

## Background
Users need a guide that takes them from zero to productive quickly, then provides depth for advanced usage. The guide should be standalone and not require reading source code.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
docs/console/user-guide.md

### Document Structure

#### 1. Introduction
- What the console provides
- When to use it (human observation)
- When not to use it (automated pipelines)
- Quick comparison: with vs without console

#### 2. Quick Start (5 minutes)
- Add dependency with feature flag
- Create console and attach to session
- Run first query and see output
- Complete code example

#### 3. Output Modes
- Rich mode: full formatting and colors
- Plain mode: text only, no ANSI
- Auto mode: detects environment
- Force mode: override detection
- Environment variables reference

#### 4. Theme Configuration
- Default themes (dark, light)
- Custom theme creation
- Color reference table
- Example themed outputs

#### 5. Renderables Catalog
- ErrorPanel: displaying errors beautifully
- QueryResultTable: formatting query results
- SchemaTree: database structure visualization
- TableInfo: single table details
- MigrationStatus: migration tracking
- Progress components: bars, spinners, trackers

#### 6. Integration Patterns
- Per-session console
- Global console
- Shared console across sessions
- Mixing console and non-console code

#### 7. Agent Compatibility
- How auto-detection works
- Environment variables for agents
- Writing agent-compatible code
- Testing with agent mode

#### 8. Troubleshooting
- Colors not showing
- Output going to wrong stream
- Performance issues
- Feature flag problems

### Writing Guidelines
- Action-oriented headings
- Code examples for every concept
- Screenshots of visual output
- Before/after comparisons
- Agent considerations throughout

## Verification Steps
1. Guide covers all major features
2. Code examples compile and run
3. Screenshots match current output
4. Technical accuracy verified
5. Newcomer can follow quick start
6. Advanced users find depth needed

## Dependencies
- All console features complete
- Visual examples for screenshots
- API stable","acceptance_criteria":"User guide covers installation and setup
Guide explains all console features with examples
Guide includes troubleshooting section
Guide explains agent compatibility
Guide is linked from main README
Guide follows project documentation style","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:17:12.944677678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:12.244654870Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:12.244533845Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pqg","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-pqg","depends_on_id":"bd-9b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qv5","title":"Implement Session struct and lifecycle management","description":"# Task: Implement Session Struct and Lifecycle Management

## Context
The Session is the central unit of work manager. It holds a database connection, tracks objects, and coordinates flushing changes to the database. This is the most critical component of the ORM layer.

## Design Philosophy
- **Explicit over implicit**: No autoflush by default (unlike Python SQLAlchemy)
- **Ownership clarity**: Session owns or borrows connection
- **Type erasure**: Identity map stores Box<dyn Any> for heterogeneous objects
- **Transaction safety**: Atomic commit/rollback semantics

## Target API
\`\`\`rust
// Create session from pool
let session = Session::new(pool.acquire().await?);

// Add new objects (will be INSERTed on flush)
session.add(&hero);

// Get by primary key (uses identity map)
let hero = session.get::<Hero>(1).await?;

// Mark for deletion
session.delete(&hero);

// Flush pending changes to DB
session.flush().await?;

// Commit the transaction
session.commit().await?;
\`\`\`

## What to Implement

### 1. Session Struct
\`\`\`rust
// In new crate: sqlmodel-session/src/lib.rs

use std::any::{Any, TypeId};
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct Session {
    /// The database connection
    connection: ConnectionHolder,
    
    /// Active transaction (if any)
    transaction: Option<Transaction>,
    
    /// Identity map: (TypeId, PK) -> tracked object
    identity_map: HashMap<ObjectKey, TrackedObject>,
    
    /// Objects marked as new (need INSERT)
    pending_new: Vec<ObjectKey>,
    
    /// Objects marked as deleted (need DELETE)
    pending_delete: Vec<ObjectKey>,
    
    /// Configuration
    config: SessionConfig,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Eq)]
struct ObjectKey {
    type_id: TypeId,
    pk_hash: u64,
}

struct TrackedObject {
    object: Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>,
    original_state: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    state: ObjectState,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
enum ObjectState {
    New,        // Needs INSERT
    Persistent, // Loaded from DB
    Deleted,    // Needs DELETE
    Detached,   // Removed from session
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Core Methods
\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn new(pool: &Pool) -> Result<Self>;
    pub fn from_connection(conn: impl Connection + 'static) -> Self;
    pub fn add<T: Model>(&mut self, obj: &T);
    pub fn delete<T: Model>(&mut self, obj: &T);
    pub async fn get<T: Model>(&mut self, pk: impl Into<Value>) -> Result<Option<T>>;
    pub async fn begin(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
    pub async fn flush(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
    pub async fn commit(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
    pub async fn rollback(&mut self) -> Result<()>;
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Query Integration
\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    pub fn query<T: Model>(&self) -> SessionQueryBuilder<T>;
}

pub struct SessionQueryBuilder<'s, T: Model> {
    session: &'s Session,
    inner: SelectBuilder<T>,
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Create/Modify
- Create: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/Cargo.toml\`
- Create: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/lib.rs\`
- Create: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/session.rs\`
- Create: \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/tracked.rs\`
- Modify: \`Cargo.toml\` (workspace members)

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (in session/src/tests.rs)

1. **Session Creation Tests**
   - \`test_session_from_connection_initializes_empty\`: No tracked objects, no pending ops
   - \`test_session_new_acquires_from_pool\`: Pool.acquire() called
   - \`test_session_config_defaults\`: Verify default SessionConfig values

2. **Object Tracking Tests**
   - \`test_add_tracks_new_object\`: add(&hero) puts hero in identity_map with ObjectState::New
   - \`test_add_same_object_twice_is_noop\`: Second add() does nothing
   - \`test_add_object_with_different_pk_tracks_separately\`: Two heroes tracked independently
   - \`test_delete_marks_persistent_for_deletion\`: delete(&hero) sets ObjectState::Deleted
   - \`test_delete_new_object_removes_from_pending\`: delete() on new object just removes it

3. **Identity Map Tests**
   - \`test_get_returns_cached_object\`: get::<Hero>(1) returns same object
   - \`test_get_fetches_from_db_if_not_cached\`: get() queries DB on cache miss
   - \`test_get_returns_none_for_nonexistent\`: get::<Hero>(999) returns None
   - \`test_get_same_pk_returns_same_instance\`: Identity guarantee

4. **Transaction Tests**
   - \`test_begin_starts_transaction\`: begin() creates Transaction
   - \`test_begin_twice_is_noop\`: Second begin() does nothing
   - \`test_commit_finalizes_transaction\`: commit() calls tx.commit()
   - \`test_rollback_discards_changes\`: rollback() calls tx.rollback()
   - \`test_commit_without_begin_is_ok\`: Implicit transaction handling

5. **Flush Tests**
   - \`test_flush_executes_inserts\`: New objects get INSERT statements
   - \`test_flush_executes_deletes\`: Deleted objects get DELETE statements
   - \`test_flush_executes_updates\`: Dirty objects get UPDATE statements
   - \`test_flush_order_delete_insert_update\`: Correct ordering prevents FK violations
   - \`test_flush_clears_pending_lists\`: pending_new/pending_delete cleared after flush

6. **State Transition Tests**
   - \`test_new_to_persistent_after_commit\`: ObjectState::New -> Persistent
   - \`test_deleted_removed_from_map_after_commit\`: Deleted objects gone
   - \`test_rollback_reverts_to_original_state\`: Original snapshot restored

### Integration Tests (tests/session_integration.rs)

1. **Full Lifecycle Tests**
   - \`test_add_flush_commit_persists_to_db\`: Full workflow with real DB
   - \`test_get_loads_from_db_into_identity_map\`: Query populates cache
   - \`test_modify_flush_updates_db\`: Change tracked object, flush, verify DB
   - \`test_delete_flush_removes_from_db\`: Delete, flush, verify gone

2. **Concurrency Tests**
   - \`test_two_sessions_same_object\`: Isolation between sessions
   - \`test_session_refresh_from_db\`: Reload object from DB

3. **Error Handling Tests**
   - \`test_commit_after_connection_error\`: Graceful failure
   - \`test_flush_with_constraint_violation\`: FK error handling
   - \`test_rollback_after_partial_flush\`: Atomicity guarantee

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/session_e2e.rs)

\`\`\`rust
/// E2E: Full Session workflow
/// 
/// Tests complete unit-of-work pattern:
/// 1. Create session from pool
/// 2. Add multiple objects
/// 3. Query with identity map
/// 4. Modify objects
/// 5. Delete some objects
/// 6. Flush all changes
/// 7. Commit transaction
/// 8. Verify database state
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_session_full_workflow() {
    // Detailed logging at each step
    tracing::info!("Starting E2E session workflow test");
    
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Step 1: Add new objects
    let team = Team { id: None, name: "Justice League".into() };
    session.add(&team);
    tracing::debug!(pending_new = ?session.pending_new_count(), "After add");
    
    // Step 2: Flush to get auto-generated IDs
    session.flush().await.unwrap();
    
    // Step 3: Verify identity map
    let cached = session.get::<Team>(1).await.unwrap();
    assert!(cached.is_some());
    
    // ... continue with full workflow
}

/// E2E: Multi-model relationships through Session
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_session_with_relationships() {
    // Create Team, Hero referencing Team
    // Verify FK constraints respected
    // Verify delete ordering (children first)
}

/// E2E: Session rollback scenario
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_session_rollback() {
    // Add objects, modify, then rollback
    // Verify DB unchanged
    // Verify session state reverted
}
\`\`\`

### Logging Requirements

\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    #[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(self, obj))]
    pub fn add<T: Model>(&mut self, obj: &T) {
        let key = ObjectKey::from(obj);
        tracing::info!(
            model = std::any::type_name::<T>(),
            pk = ?key.pk_hash,
            "Adding object to session"
        );
        // ...
    }
    
    #[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(self))]
    pub async fn flush(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        tracing::info!(
            inserts = self.pending_new.len(),
            deletes = self.pending_delete.len(),
            "Starting flush"
        );
        
        let start = std::time::Instant::now();
        // ... flush logic ...
        
        tracing::info!(
            elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis(),
            "Flush completed"
        );
        Ok(())
    }
    
    #[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(self))]
    pub async fn commit(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        tracing::info!("Committing transaction");
        // ...
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Debug Diagnostics

Add diagnostic methods for debugging:

\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    /// Get count of objects pending INSERT
    pub fn pending_new_count(&self) -> usize { self.pending_new.len() }
    
    /// Get count of objects pending DELETE
    pub fn pending_delete_count(&self) -> usize { self.pending_delete.len() }
    
    /// Get total tracked object count
    pub fn tracked_count(&self) -> usize { self.identity_map.len() }
    
    /// Dump session state for debugging
    pub fn debug_state(&self) -> SessionDebugInfo {
        SessionDebugInfo {
            tracked: self.tracked_count(),
            pending_new: self.pending_new_count(),
            pending_delete: self.pending_delete_count(),
            has_transaction: self.transaction.is_some(),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session struct implemented with all fields
- [ ] SessionConfig with sensible defaults
- [ ] add(), delete(), get() methods working
- [ ] begin(), commit(), rollback() transaction management
- [ ] flush() executes pending operations in correct order
- [ ] Identity map correctly caches objects
- [ ] Query integration returns tracked objects
- [ ] Tracing instrumentation at debug/info levels
- [ ] Debug diagnostics methods implemented
- [ ] Unit tests: 20+ test cases passing
- [ ] Integration tests: 6+ tests with real DB
- [ ] E2E tests: 3 full workflow tests passing
- [ ] Documentation with API examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:18:53.735172914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:30:16.654434530Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T21:30:16.654355523Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qv5","depends_on_id":"bd-369","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:18:53.746434875Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s6v","title":"Integrate console into sqlmodel-sqlite driver","description":"## Purpose
Add rich console output to the SQLite driver for database operations, PRAGMA visualization, and transaction state feedback.

## Background
SQLite operations that benefit from console output:
- Database file open/create
- Attaching additional databases
- PRAGMA queries and results
- WAL mode operations
- Transaction state (begin/commit/rollback)
- Busy timeout waiting

## Implementation Details

### File Modifications
crates/sqlmodel-sqlite/src/connection.rs
crates/sqlmodel-sqlite/src/lib.rs (re-exports)

### ConsoleAware Implementation
Add optional SqlModelConsole to SqliteConnection:
struct SqliteConnection {
    // existing fields...
    console: Option<Arc<SqlModelConsole>>,
}

### Database Open Feedback
SQLite database: ./data.db
  Mode: read-write
  Journal: WAL
  Page size: 4096
  Cache size: 2000 pages

### PRAGMA Visualization
Format PRAGMA query results in a nice table:
PRAGMA table_info(users):
+---------+---------+---------+---------+
| cid     | name    | type    | notnull |
+---------+---------+---------+---------+
| 0       | id      | INTEGER | 1       |
| 1       | name    | TEXT    | 0       |
+---------+---------+---------+---------+

### Transaction State
Show transaction boundaries:
[BEGIN] Transaction started
  ... operations ...
[COMMIT] Transaction committed (23ms, 5 operations)

### WAL Checkpoint Progress
For checkpoint operations:
WAL checkpoint: [========>      ] 60% (1200/2000 pages)

### Busy Timeout Feedback
When waiting for locks:
[..] Waiting for database lock... (2.1s)

## Plain Text Mode
Opened SQLite database: ./data.db (WAL mode)
Transaction committed: 23ms
PRAGMA table_info: 5 columns

## Conditional Compilation
Use cfg feature flag same as postgres driver.

## Verification Steps
1. Test database open with console enabled
2. Test PRAGMA formatting
3. Test transaction state feedback
4. Verify WAL checkpoint progress
5. Test busy timeout messaging
6. Verify plain text output
7. Test with console disabled

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-console crate
- SqlModelConsole, Theme components","acceptance_criteria":"SQLite driver implements ConsoleAware trait

Error states display through ErrorPanel
All unit tests verify console integration
Integration tests verify end-to-end output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:38.545378742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:05:16.917375863Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:04:31.335027616Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s6v","depends_on_id":"bd-1lv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-s6v","depends_on_id":"bd-2kb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-s6v","depends_on_id":"bd-88i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-s6v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

 {"id":"bd-tel","title":"Write sqlmodel-console Cargo.toml with feature flags","description":"# Write sqlmodel-console Cargo.toml with Feature Flags

## Task Description

Create the Cargo.toml for sqlmodel-console with proper feature flags that make
rich_rust an optional dependency. This is critical for the "zero cost when disabled"
design principle.

## Cargo.toml Content

```toml
[package]
name = "sqlmodel-console"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.85"
authors = ["Jeffrey Emanuel <jeff@pastel.network>"]
description = "Beautiful terminal output for sqlmodel_rust"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/sqlmodel_rust"
keywords = ["database", "console", "terminal", "rich", "styled"]
categories = ["command-line-interface", "visualization"]

[features]
default = []

# Basic rich output (tables, panels, colors)
rich = ["dep:rich_rust"]

# SQL syntax highlighting (adds syntect dependency via rich_rust)
syntax = ["rich", "rich_rust/syntax"]

# Full feature set
full = ["rich", "syntax"]

[dependencies]
# Core (always available)
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }

# Optional: rich terminal output
rich_rust = { path = "../../../rich_rust", optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
# For testing output capture
# (none needed initially)
```

## Key Design Decisions

### Why Path Dependency for rich_rust?
1. Both projects are developed together
2. Avoids version sync issues with crates.io
3. Local changes immediately available
4. No network dependency during development

### Why `dep:rich_rust` Syntax?
Rust 2021+ allows `dep:` prefix to reference optional dependencies in feature 
definitions without implicitly creating a feature with the same name.

### Feature Hierarchy
```
full ──► syntax ──► rich ──► [rich_rust crate]
```

Each feature level adds capabilities:
- `rich`: Basic Console, colors, tables, panels, trees
- `syntax`: SQL syntax highlighting via syntect
- `full`: All features enabled

## Verification

```bash
# Check Cargo.toml syntax
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console 2>&1 || echo "Expected: won't work until workspace updated"

# After workspace update:
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features rich
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features syntax
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console --features full
```

## Notes

- Path to rich_rust is `../../../rich_rust` because:
  - From `crates/sqlmodel-console/` we go up to `crates/`
  - Then up to `sqlmodel_rust/`
  - Then up to parent containing both projects
  - Then into `rich_rust/`
  - Adjust if your directory structure differs

- No workspace inheritance for dependencies here since rich_rust is local","acceptance_criteria":"Cargo.toml has correct package metadata
Feature flags defined (rich, syntax, full)
rich_rust is optional path dependency
serde is optional for theme serialization
Dependencies version-locked appropriately
Package compiles with all feature combinations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:03:05.244443813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:44.134814640Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:44.134741532Z","close_reason":"Cargo.toml already exists with feature flags (rich, syntax, full) as specified in the task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","phase-1","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tel","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-tel","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-u12","title":"Phase 4: Query Output System","description":"# Phase 4: Query Output System

## Overview

This phase implements beautiful query result display - tables for result sets,
SQL syntax highlighting for generated queries, and query structure visualization.

## Why Query Output Matters

Query results are the primary output of a database library. Beautiful result
tables transform the experience from "reading raw data" to "understanding data."

### Before (Plain)
```
id|name|email|created_at
1|Alice|alice@example.com|2024-01-15 10:30:00
2|Bob|bob@example.com|2024-01-16 14:22:00
```

### After (Rich)
```
┌──────────────────────────── Query Results ────────────────────────────┐
│ 2 rows in 12.34ms                                                     │
├───────┬─────────────┬──────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┤
│    id │ name        │ email                │ created_at              │
├───────┼─────────────┼──────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┤
│     1 │ Alice       │ alice@example.com    │ 2024-01-15 10:30:00    │
│     2 │ Bob         │ bob@example.com      │ 2024-01-16 14:22:00    │
└───────┴─────────────┴──────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┘
```

## Components to Implement

### 1. QueryResultTable Renderable
A table specifically for query results with:
- Auto-sized columns based on content
- Type-based cell coloring (nulls gray, numbers cyan, etc.)
- Row count and timing in header/footer
- Truncation for long values
- Optional row numbers

### 2. Value Type Styling
Color values based on their SQL type:
- `NULL` → Gray, italic
- `Boolean` → Yellow
- `Integer/Float` → Cyan
- `String` → Green
- `Date/Time` → Magenta
- `Binary/Blob` → Orange (show "[BLOB: 1234 bytes]")
- `JSON` → Purple

### 3. SQL Syntax Highlighting
Use rich_rust's syntax highlighting for SQL:
- Keywords (SELECT, FROM, WHERE) → Magenta
- Strings → Green
- Numbers → Purple
- Comments → Gray
- Operators → Red
- Identifiers → White

### 4. Query Builder Visualization
Tree view of query structure:
```
SELECT from heroes
├── Columns: id, name, secret_name
├── WHERE: age > 18
├── ORDER BY: name ASC
└── LIMIT: 10
```

### 5. Query Timing Display
Visual breakdown of query execution:
```
Query completed in 12.34ms (3 rows)
  Parse:  ██░░░░░░░░ 1.2ms
  Plan:   ████░░░░░░ 3.4ms  
  Exec:   ████████░░ 7.7ms
```

## Plain Mode Output Formats

For agent compatibility, plain mode supports:
1. **Pipe-delimited**: `id|name|email` (default)
2. **CSV**: Standard CSV with quoting
3. **JSON Lines**: One JSON object per row
4. **JSON Array**: Single array of objects

The format is selectable via `QueryResultTable::format()`.

## Tasks in This Phase

1. Create QueryResultTable renderable
2. Implement Value type styling system
3. Integrate SQL syntax highlighting
4. Create QueryTreeView for query structure
5. Implement QueryTiming display
6. Add methods to sqlmodel-query Select/Insert/Update/Delete
7. Create examples and tests

## Integration with sqlmodel-query

Add console display methods to query builders:

```rust
impl<M: Model> Select<M> {
    /// Print the generated SQL with syntax highlighting.
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub fn explain(&self, console: &SqlModelConsole, dialect: Dialect) {
        let sql = self.to_sql(dialect);
        console.print_sql(&sql);
    }
    
    /// Print query structure as tree.
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub fn print_structure(&self, console: &SqlModelConsole) {
        let tree = self.to_tree();
        console.print_tree(&tree);
    }
}
```

## Dependencies (Beads)

This phase depends on:
- Phase 2 (Core Infrastructure) - SqlModelConsole
- Phase 3 (Error Display) - for error handling in display

## Performance Considerations

- Column width calculation should be O(rows × cols)
- Cache column widths after first calculation
- Truncate long values rather than wrapping (configurable)
- Limit displayed rows (e.g., first 100) with "... and N more" indicator","acceptance_criteria":"QueryResultTable renderable displays query results in table format

 {"id":"bd-vz2","title":"Integrate console into sqlmodel-postgres driver","description":"## Purpose
Add rich console output to the PostgreSQL driver for connection progress, authentication feedback, and query visualization.

## Background
PostgreSQL connections involve multiple steps:
1. DNS resolution
2. TCP connection
3. SSL/TLS negotiation (optional)
4. Authentication (password, SCRAM-SHA-256, etc)
5. Parameter negotiation

Each step can be visualized to show progress and help debug connection issues.

## Implementation Details

### File Modifications
crates/sqlmodel-postgres/src/connection.rs
crates/sqlmodel-postgres/src/lib.rs (re-exports)

### ConsoleAware Implementation
Add optional SqlModelConsole to PostgresConnection:
struct PostgresConnection {
    // existing fields...
    console: Option<Arc<SqlModelConsole>>,
}

impl ConsoleAware for PostgresConnection {
    fn set_console(&mut self, console: Option<Arc<SqlModelConsole>>) {
        self.console = console;
    }
}

### Connection Progress Output
When console is Some and mode is Rich:

Connecting to PostgreSQL...
  [OK] DNS resolved: 192.168.1.100
  [OK] TCP connected
  [..] Negotiating SSL...
  [OK] SSL established (TLS 1.3)
  [..] Authenticating (SCRAM-SHA-256)...
  [OK] Authenticated as user
  [OK] Connected (server: PostgreSQL 15.2)

### Query Timing Output
For queries, optionally show timing:
Query executed in 12.3ms (5 rows returned)

### EXPLAIN Visualization
When EXPLAIN or EXPLAIN ANALYZE is run:
- Parse the explain output
- Render as a tree showing:
  - Node types (Seq Scan, Index Scan, Hash Join)
  - Costs and actual times
  - Row estimates vs actual
  - Highlighting expensive nodes

### COPY Progress
For COPY operations:
- Show progress bar with row count
- Display throughput (rows/sec, MB/s)

## Plain Text Mode
In plain mode, emit minimal text:
Connected to PostgreSQL 15.2 at localhost:5432
Query: 12.3ms, 5 rows

## Conditional Compilation
Use cfg feature flag:
#[cfg(feature = "console")]
fn emit_connection_progress(&self, stage: &str, status: StageStatus) { ... }

#[cfg(not(feature = "console"))]
fn emit_connection_progress(&self, _: &str, _: StageStatus) {}

## Verification Steps
1. Test connection with console enabled
2. Test connection with console disabled
3. Verify agent mode produces plain text only
4. Test EXPLAIN tree rendering
5. Test COPY progress
6. Verify SSL and auth step feedback
7. Test connection failure messaging

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-console crate
- SqlModelConsole, Theme, OperationProgress components","acceptance_criteria":"PostgreSQL driver implements ConsoleAware trait
Connection events emit console output
Query execution outputs to console when enabled
Error states display through ErrorPanel
All unit tests verify console integration
Integration tests verify end-to-end output","notes":"Console integration for PostgreSQL driver implemented with ConsoleAware trait, ConnectionStage enum, emit_progress/emit_connected methods, conditional compilation via 'console' feature flag, all 56 tests passing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:25.175877255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:01:07.349609007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:01:07.349523175Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vz2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-vz2","depends_on_id":"bd-2kb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-vz2","depends_on_id":"bd-88i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"bd-vz2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement
